evals.prompt:
Review this function for style issues:

```python
async def get_user_affinity(global_user_id, db=None):
    try:
        if db is None:
            from kazusa_ai_chatbot.db import get_db
            db = await get_db()
        doc = await db.user_profiles.find_one({"global_user_id": global_user_id})
        affinity = doc.get("affinity", 500)
        logger.info("Got affinity %d for user %s", affinity, global_user_id)
        return affinity
    except Exception:
        return 500
```
evals.expected_output: Identifies all four violations: inline import (Rule 1), over-broad except Exception (Rule 3), scattered default 500 via .get() (Rule 5), try-except swallowing an internal DB failure (Rule 6). Also flags missing docstring (Rule 4). Proposes corrected version.

assertions.name: flags_inline_import
assertions.description: Response identifies the inline import inside the function body as a Rule 1 violation

assertions.name: flags_broad_except
assertions.description: Response flags 'except Exception' as too broad and a Rule 3 violation

assertions.name: flags_get_default
assertions.description: Response flags doc.get('affinity', 500) as a Rule 5 violation — default should live at write time

assertions.name: flags_swallowed_db_error
assertions.description: Response identifies that catching Exception and returning 500 silences a DB failure (Rule 6)

assertions.name: flags_missing_docstring
assertions.description: Response flags the missing docstring as a Rule 4 violation

assertions.name: provides_corrected_code
assertions.description: Response includes a corrected version of the function with all violations fixed

evals.prompt: Write a function called `classify_message_depth` that takes a user message string, a topic string, and an affinity integer, calls an LLM to decide if the message is SHALLOW or DEEP, and returns a dict with 'depth' and 'reasoning'. The LLM might return broken JSON.
evals.expected_output: Produces code that: imports at top level, has a full docstring with Args/Returns, wraps only json.loads in try/except json.JSONDecodeError (not the whole LLM call), does not use .get() with defaults on internal state, and does not use bare except.

assertions.name: no_inline_imports
assertions.description: All imports are at module level, not inside the function

assertions.name: has_complete_docstring
assertions.description: Function has docstring with purpose, Args section, and Returns section

assertions.name: try_wraps_only_json_parse
assertions.description: The try block contains only the JSON parsing statement, not the entire LLM call chain

assertions.name: except_is_specific
assertions.description: except clause names json.JSONDecodeError or equivalent specific type, not Exception

assertions.name: no_swallowed_llm_call_errors
assertions.description: The LLM ainvoke() call is not wrapped in a try-except that silences failures

evals.expected_output: Explains why the tech lead is right: (1) inline imports (Rule 1), (2) try wraps the whole function body instead of minimum range (Rule 2), (3) except Exception is too broad (Rule 3), (4) no docstring (Rule 4), (5) .get() with defaults on internal state (Rule 5), (6) swallowing DB errors hides bugs (Rule 6). Provides corrected version where the try-except is removed entirely since there is no external I/O that justifies it.

assertions.name: moves_imports_to_top
assertions.description: Fixed code moves the inline imports to the module top level

assertions.name: removes_catch_all_try_except
assertions.description: Fixed code removes or significantly narrows the try-except block; internal DB calls are not wrapped

assertions.name: adds_docstring
assertions.description: Fixed code includes a docstring with purpose, Args, and Returns

assertions.name: explains_get_default_issue
assertions.description: Response explains that state.get('new_facts', []) and state.get('affinity_delta', 0) mask missing upstream writes

assertions.name: no_success_false_return
assertions.description: Fixed code does not return a success/failure dict that silences exceptions — errors should propagate
